unexpected_finds.title: Substrate-citation contamination pattern recurrence at venue-identifier tier (AAR)
unexpected_finds.description: PROMPT 205 introduced 'AAR (Archive of Automated Reasoning)' as a venue-identifier through CLI agent drafting. Synth rubber-duck QA caught the issue at fire-receive time. Web-verification confirmed AAR is not a recognized peer-reviewed journal. This is the third documented class of substrate-citation hallucination in the project after DOIs/arXiv-IDs (031 case) and bridge SHAs (105 case).

pattern_taxonomy.tier: venue-identifier (new tier; was previously DOI/arXiv-ID + bridge-SHA)
pattern_taxonomy.preventive_rule_analog: DOI/arXiv-ID pre-verification rule (post-031 verdict; standing instruction)

unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_status: PROMOTION RECOMMENDED — new fact: 'Venue identifiers (journal acronyms, full names) in relay prompts require pre-verification analog to DOI/arXiv-ID rule; AAR=Archive of Automated Reasoning is one documented hallucinated venue (not peer-reviewed)'
unexpected_finds.discovery_credit: synth (Claude solo-witness) — analogous pattern to W20 068 rubber-duck QA case (operator parallel-QA + synth complementary catching)
unexpected_finds.remediation_scope: triage matrix v1.5 + L2 pivot v1.2 + PROMPT 205 prompt renamed (original AAR text preserved as historical record)

unexpected_finds.title: Q-205-9 single-highest-leverage CLI prior FLIPPED by synth (Item 21 over Item 18)
unexpected_finds.description: CLI nominated Item 18 SIARC PDE → JFR (post-sorry-reduction) as single highest-leverage redirect on the basis that closing the JAR-cluster question definitively would generate the most useful information. Synth verdict: Item 21 Tunnell CNP → JFR (no pre-resubmit work; manuscript + deposit stable; can fire within 72h) is higher-leverage per leverage = information-per-unit-effort criterion. CLI prior is now superseded.
unexpected_finds.calibration_note: This is the second CLI-prior flip captured in PROMPT 205 absorption (the first being Q-205-3 JNT→JTNB venue order). Two flips out of nine questions = 22% flip rate; consistent with healthy synth-as-T1 dual-witness function rather than CLI epistemic drift.
unexpected_finds.downstream_implication: Item 21 fire → 72h actionable per verdict; Item 18 fire deferred to 2-4 week DELAYED tier
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_status: OBSERVED — pattern; no promotion needed (already covered by existing 'rubber-duck QA discipline' memory cluster)

unexpected_finds.title: Adamczewski-editor portfolio-wide check pattern
unexpected_finds.description: Adamczewski rejected Item 22 (JTNB content-mute 6 May 2026) and is being deferred for arXiv-endorsement outreach 6+ months (Q-205-6 LOCK). Synth Q-205-3 RISK_FLAG further extends this: Adamczewski-editor-check required at JTNB BEFORE Item 16 redirect fire (if Adamczewski handles continued-fractions / Diophantine content at JTNB, swap primary to JNT or Bull. LMS). The pattern generalizes: a known-recent-rejector editor should be excluded as both (a) re-submission editor at their journal AND (b) endorsement-outreach contact for 6-12 month cooling period.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_status: OBSERVED — operational discipline; may promote if pattern recurs at second editor
